title: Multi Agent Sentiment Tool (Grounded in real results)
author: fibspeak
selftext:
We all known sentiment indicators are most often used as counter signals. The greed/fear index etc, these are very unreliable to follow as true sentiment guides. 

I wanted to make one based on real strategies backed with real results. 

  
Here's how I did it; 

  
1 - To assess the reliability of a strategy we first have to define how it should perform. The first thing I did was sync up setups with strategies and give these strategies an expected win rate, RR and max DD. For a trade to qualify to be part of sentiment ranking it has to have a stop, target and entry but it also has to pre-state the expected results on the strategy.  This is important for two reasons, which I'll cover later. 

  
2 - Create LLM personas that can all access a common research lib, common set of strategies and common tools to run and manage the strategies. Because of the requirements of step one this means they submit the trades with all the KPIs needed to tracking them with the tools to submit a trade needing to capture all of these to be able to run. Forcing the LLMs into a common schema but letting them interoperate the same data in different ways based on their persona style.  

  
3 - The LLMs start to generate posts and signals and these are fed into end to end tracking. Tracking avoids vanity metrics and focuses on the real core points of expected profitability and sustainability in markets. All risk is normalised to "R" forcing everything to be automatically risk adjusted, which is the only honest way of tracking performance. 

  
4 - This is where step 1 becomes critical. Obviously we can rank our LLM personas based on their PL outcomes. The amount of units of risk they made vrs the amount they drew down but to make a truly reflective sentiment indicator we need to know how accurate these were as per their forecast.  Since we forced all trades to have their expected win rate and RR stated before the fact, after some trading we can rank not only PL but how well calibrated the real outcome was to the probability forecast (brier). 

  
5 - Now we have the working parts of a sentiment indicator. We can filter out any profiles with bad results or results not matching what was expected. Dropping the weak results from the sentiment. Then with the remaining ones we have their expected win rate, the amount of "R" they are betting (a confidence indicator) and how reliable they have historically been with their forecast/result statistics.  

https://preview.redd.it/dpxc2y47g33h1.png?width=1816&format=png&auto=webp&s=c899e86943f0c1d48ef7f561e4da343cf2eebe44

  
Now we have a sentiment indicator that is made up of the real positions the LLM personas are taking with that cross referenced against their historic performance to weight how important they are in the sentiment ranking.  This can be done on all individual assets being traded. 

  
And all individual assets being trading can be converted into an overall risk on/off indicator. 

https://preview.redd.it/ch8nuzlog33h1.png?width=1735&format=png&auto=webp&s=f1c73d768c14ca35ddc071993d992dfd3c39fde5

  
Since we have extremely (historically) reliable strats on the profiles posting trades we have strong sentiment indicators on anything being traded. 

We can then start to assign scores to the risk on/off profile of different things. If our reliable trades are long 3R with 50% expected win rate on SPX this is risk on, but not massively. However, if they are trading 10R with 75% expected win rate and also long 3R doge with 50% expected win rate and we also have short sentiment on the safe haven currencies we then have a very strong risk on rating. 

  
Unlike other sentiment indictors this is not based on the averaging of unprofitable traders (as traditional volume based sentiment is). 

It's not based on a collection of lagging indicators (as things like the fear and green index is). 

The sentiment is based exclusively on the forecast of ranked performant personas. 

All of these are using support and resistance based analysis to aim to forecast where the future important levels will be, not aggregate info on what just happened.
